title: Split LCL color discrepency in EoE red v orange
display_title: Split LCL color discrepency in EoE red v orange
wikitext:
[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Mar 19, 2015 7:46 pm Bagheera wrote: Lilith. She's clearly spewing it all over the place.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Mar 19, 2015 10:17 pm Reichu wrote: EoE complicates the issue a bit, since a great deal of the LCL seen is in fact red. (The LCL Sea, the source of the stuff, is also overwhelmingly red.) Of course no reason for this discrepancy is provided, but it makes it impossible to say that GNR isn't bleeding LCL anymore. Not that it matters as far as the ocean is concerned, since GNR's bloodstream apparently emptied into orbit. If ya'll will notice, her spontaneous disembodiment is entirely bloodless. All of the 'blood' seen at that point is what spilled onto her from the Black Moon.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Mar 20, 2015 5:45 am Sachi wrote: What you describe occurs during the Instrumentality sequence

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Mar 21, 2015 12:23 am I'm actually now sorta intrigued by this decision to change the color of LCL from orange to red in certain instance when it is depicted. Does anybody have more information regarding this so that I can fankwank over it?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Mar 21, 2015 2:05 pm The only time I remember LCL being red is after impact events, like the Dead Sea of Antarctica, and after the Rei Collective abducts the entire human race. Could that have something to do with it? Either A) Impact events chemically alter LCL in some way, or B) There's just SO MUCH of the damn stuff it turns red when you get a minimum of continent-amounts of it?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 21, 2015 2:23 pm The South Pole is more magenta than red, truthfully. That case is also complicated by the fact that the region underwent some kind of "Adam-based ecosystem" environmental reformatting. I personally doubt the effects were contained to the atmosphere -- i.e., the ocean might not actually be water anymore -- but who knows. My preferred fanwank for red LCL is that souls dissolved at a high enough saturation within LCL become visible to the extent of tinting the fluid red. Assuming that human instrumentality only involved human souls, and all the rest stayed behind on Earth, there would have been plenty enough souls to do the necessary tinting. This may even explain the red color acquired by the landmasses. The continents are normally coated in biomass, and 3I would have reverted all this biomass to LCL. To simplify the process of making background paintings for the film, things like short- and long-term LCL distribution patterns across the land aren't taken into consideration, and it's just assumed that a significant enough film of LCL lays upon the land (tinted by the souls that subsequently dropped into it) to make the ground look red from an aerial view. Or maybe there's a simpler explanation I'm not thinking of. EDIT: Taking a quick look in the storyboards... C367 (Cut of "red effect" spreading across globe and crosses popping up. Immediately after harpies kill themselves.) "The surface is being stained with LCL's red color. Particles of red light flutter about." C427 (Cut right after Eva-01 spreads her wings and we see souls spreading away from GNR) "The LCL on the surface turns the color of red blood." (This is before the Black Moon is opened and the souls released. Why wasn't this LCL already red? I dunno lol.) When GNR's body parts drop, the displaced fluid is called "columns of blood". The view of the entire Earth toward the end says that the planet is "stained red with blood".

[]:             Quote Post by SawItAtAge10 » Wed Mar 25, 2015 2:19 pm The yellowish/orange LCL always kind of reminded me of egg yolk from like a still born while the red LCL kind of looks like blood... With that in mind, could it be that the yellowish orange LCL is a "purer" or "fresh" form while the deeper shades of red have "coagulated" or "rotted"? This analogy would certainly explain why the antarctic was a metaphorical "dead sea". What I don't get is when the LCL is a glittery pinkish color (as seen when the Evas are on stand by).

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Mar 25, 2015 3:12 pm Apropos of nothing, when I said she was "spewing it all over the place" I wasn't talking about when GNR died (though I was anything but clear about this point). I was actually referring to her time on the cross, when she was producing the stuff 24/7 for years. And of course my clarification of my initial statement that gave rise to this tangent is now off-topic since said tangent is now a separate thread . . .
summary:
